_note: CONSTRUCTED evaluation scenario for H7 (defeasible reconciliation). State/local claims are illustrative fixtures written in the style of the cited manuals. This directory is intentionally separate from seed_data/rules/ and is never seeded into, or counted toward, the verified rule corpus.
description: Three-tier hierarchy on one parameter: federal minimum shoulder (2 ft), state collector minimum (3 ft), county ordinance minimum (6 ft). The local rule defeats both upstream tiers by jurisdiction priority; a 4-ft shoulder passes federal and state but fails the governing county requirement.

claims.name: Minimum Usable Shoulder (AASHTO)
claims.citation: AASHTO Green Book, 7th Ed., Ch. 4 (Cross-Section Elements)
claims.description: National default: usable shoulder at least 2 ft.

claims.name: Rural Collector Shoulder (WisDOT, constructed)
claims.description: State supplement: rural collectors carry at least 3-ft paved shoulders.

claims.name: County Highway Shoulder Ordinance (constructed)
claims.description: Local ordinance: county-owned highways require 6-ft shoulders for farm equipment.
